choices.text: Attribution credits an ad for sales within its window (last-touch + view-through). Incrementality measures sales that wouldn't have happened without the ad. They diverge most for retargeting (high attributed ROAS, low lift — buyers were returning anyway) and branded search (high credit, near-zero incrementality — users typing brand were buying regardless). Most accounts overstate true contribution by 10-30%; lift tests ground the real number.

questions.explanation: Attribution = credit; incrementality = causation. Retargeting + branded search usually overcredited.

questions.prompt: First lift test shows retargeting at 0.5x incremental ROAS vs prospecting at 1.8x incremental. Action?

choices.text: Reallocate budget toward prospecting (and away from retargeting), which is delivering more genuine new revenue per dollar. Retargeting at 0.5x incremental means the ads are mostly re-claiming credit for sales that would have happened anyway. Shift a meaningful portion (often 20-40% of retargeting budget) to prospecting and retest in 6-12 months. Don't kill retargeting entirely — some lift exists — but rebalance to where the causal money is.

questions.explanation: Reallocate toward prospecting; retargeting was claiming inevitable sales.
